title: My pen pal in another country
summary: Reflect on writing letters or messages to a pen pal in a different country, using present simple to describe the habit.
topic: Writing to a pen pal in a different country
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a pen pal (or someone you contact regularly in another country). Describe how you communicate, what you usually talk about, and what you have learned from them. Use present simple and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: My pen pal's name is… and she / he lives in…
We usually write to each other…
I always learn something new because…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bạn thư / bạn trao đổi thư từ

sampleText: My pen pal's name is Fatima and she lives in Casablanca, Morocco. We write to each other every two weeks. I write in English and she writes in English too, but sometimes she adds a few words in Arabic and I always try to learn them. We usually talk about everyday life. I describe my city and she describes her city. I am always curious about her food and her market. She is always curious about the public transport in my city. Sometimes she sends me a photo of her street or a meal she cooked. I always reply quickly because I look forward to her messages. I sometimes translate a word in her letter with a dictionary. It is a little slow but it is interesting. I learn things about Morocco that I never see in books. Fatima makes me think about my city in a new way too. I think a pen pal is a very good way to see the world.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: adds a few words in Arabic
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thêm vào một vài từ tiếng Ả Rập
Tôi miêu tả thành phố của mình
cô ấy miêu tả thành phố của cô ấy
phương tiện giao thông công cộng
gửi cho tôi một bức ảnh
mong chờ tin nhắn của cô ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: makes me think about my city in a new way
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: khiến tôi nghĩ về thành phố của mình theo một cách mới

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third-person singular -s in the present simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi chủ ngữ là 'she', 'he', hay 'it', động từ thêm -s hoặc -es: 'she writes', 'she describes', 'she sends', 'she adds'. Đây là điểm ngữ pháp quan trọng khi viết về một người khác.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When talking about another person (she/he/it), add -s or -es to the verb: 'she writes', 'she describes', 'she sends'. This -s is easy to forget, especially when you're writing about someone else.

sampleTranslationVi: Tên bạn thư của tôi là Fatima và cô ấy sống ở Casablanca, Morocco. Chúng tôi viết thư cho nhau hai tuần một lần. Tôi viết bằng tiếng Anh và cô ấy cũng viết bằng tiếng Anh, nhưng đôi khi cô ấy thêm vào một vài từ tiếng Ả Rập và tôi luôn cố gắng học chúng. Chúng tôi thường nói về cuộc sống hàng ngày. Tôi miêu tả thành phố của mình còn cô ấy miêu tả thành phố của cô ấy. Tôi luôn tò mò về đồ ăn và chợ của cô ấy. Cô ấy luôn tò mò về phương tiện giao thông công cộng ở thành phố tôi. Đôi khi cô ấy gửi cho tôi một bức ảnh về con phố hoặc một bữa ăn cô ấy nấu. Tôi luôn trả lời nhanh vì tôi mong chờ tin nhắn của cô ấy. Đôi khi tôi dịch một từ trong thư của cô ấy bằng từ điển. Hơi chậm nhưng rất thú vị. Tôi học được những điều về Morocco mà tôi chưa từng thấy trong sách. Fatima cũng khiến tôi nghĩ về thành phố của mình theo một cách mới. Tôi nghĩ bạn thư là một cách rất hay để khám phá thế giới.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'She ___ her city in every letter.'
options: describe
describing
describes
described
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does 'she' need on a present simple verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly with the verb 'be'?
options: She is always curious about my city.
She always is curious about my city.
Always she is curious about my city.
She is curious about my always city.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'be', the adverb of frequency goes after 'is': 'is always curious'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does an adverb go when the verb is 'be'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'because' to say why you reply quickly?
options: I reply quickly because I look forward to her messages.
I reply quickly because look forward messages.
Because I look forward I reply quickly messages.
I reply quickly messages because forward look.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' + full clause: 'because I look forward to her messages'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', is there a complete clause with subject and verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says what two people usually talk about?
options: We usually talk about everyday life.
We usually talks about everyday life.
We usually talking about everyday life.
Everyday life we usually talk.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'we', the verb takes no -s: 'we talk'. 'Usually' goes before the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does the verb take with 'we'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'She ___ me a photo of her street.'
options: send
sends
sending
sended
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'she' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show a contrast?
options: It is a little slow but it is interesting.
It is a little slow but is interesting.
It is slow little but interesting it.
But it slow a little is interesting it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two contrasting clauses, each with subject + 'is' + adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need subject + verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says something you have learned from a pen pal?
options: I learn things about Morocco that I never see in books.
I learn things about Morocco that I never sees in books.
I learns things about Morocco I never see books.
Things Morocco I learn never see in books.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I learn' and 'I never see' — both present simple with 'I', no -s needed.
mcQuestions.hint: With 'I', the verb never takes -s in the present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says how often you write to each other?
options: We write to each other every two weeks.
We write to each other every two week.
We write each other every two weeks to.
We write to other each two weeks every.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'every two weeks' is correct; 'write to each other' is the standard phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'weeks' singular or plural in 'every two weeks'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says a person makes you think differently?
options: She makes me think about my city in a new way.
She makes me to think about my city in a new way.
She make me think about my city in a new way.
She makes me thinking about my city new way.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'makes me think' — causative: 'make + object + bare infinitive'; third-person singular adds -s.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'makes me', does the next verb need 'to' or is it a bare infinitive?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says you always try to learn new words?
options: I always try to learn them.
I always try learning them.
I always tries to learn them.
Always I try to learning them.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'try to + base verb' is the standard structure; 'I' takes no -s; 'always' before the main verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If you could have a pen pal anywhere in the world, which country would you choose and what is one question you would ask them about their everyday life?
